contextual_role: Boundary case-study evidence for agent-authored reusable operational/deployment artifacts in software-system development.
full_text_rationale: Full text describes Self-Defining Systems as an AI-agent workflow for infrastructure/software-system design, including a persistent agent-authored deployment playbook distilled from prior failures and reused on fresh VM deployments; it reports quantitative case-study results for two LLM runtime implementations, throughput, development time, and deployment-time reduction.
extractor_notes: Boundary include retained as contextual systems-development evidence. Do not count as an architecture study because the paper reports a broad SDS workflow/case study and an agent-authored playbook, not a dedicated persistence architecture evaluated as the study contribution.
autores: Thomas Anderson, Ratul Mahajan, Simon Peter, Luke Zettlemoyer
formato_armazenamento: Boundary SDS case study: specifications, intermediate artifacts, code, benchmark results, and an agent-authored key-solutions deployment playbook distilled from prior failures.
framework_agente: Custom Self-Defining Systems workflow for LLM-runtime development
llm_utilizado: NR for the coding agents; Qwen3-0.6B is used as the runtime benchmark workload.
benchmarks_utilizados: LLM runtime throughput benchmark on one NVIDIA L4 GPU in a GCP VM; nano-vLLM baseline; deployment-time case study.
metricas_desempenho: Throughput tokens/s, speedup since inception, development time, deployment time, human intervention.
baseline_comparado: nano-vLLM throughput, original Allmos baseline, naive PyTorch baseline, and human-in-the-loop deployment debugging.
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary cost; reports development and deployment time.
resultado_principal: allmos v2 reaches about 1.7k tokens/s, near nano-vLLM at 1.76k tokens/s and 76x faster than the original Allmos baseline. A monolithic runtime reaches 1.2k tokens/s and 53x speedup. An agent-authored key-solutions playbook reduces fresh-VM deployment from about 90 minutes of human-in-the-loop debugging to about 6 minutes without human intervention, and development time drops from 35 days to 2 days for the second runtime.
desempenho_vs_baseline: 76x speedup over original Allmos for allmos v2; 53x speedup for monolithic-llm-runtime since inception; deployment time about 90 minutes to about 6 minutes.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports time-efficiency gains rather than monetary cost.
limitacoes: White paper and ongoing case study; no controlled multi-system benchmark of SDS as a persistence architecture; agents did not exceed nano-vLLM performance and plateaued on some optimization attempts.
ameacas_validade: Single infrastructure domain, prototype workflow, substantial human framing of goals/evaluation, and no independent replication.
